site.title: Interactive Systems Learning
site.tagline: Hands-on explainers for how real infrastructure works.

modules.title: MIG vs vGPU — Internals
modules.description: How NVIDIA partitions a datacenter GPU (modelled on the H100 SXM5 80GB). Build MIG slices, vGPUs and MIG-backed vGPUs, run workloads, and watch spatial vs temporal scheduling, fault isolation and time-slicing live. Includes a ~3-minute explainer film.

modules.title: NVIDIA Infra Controller (NICo)
modules.description: Zero-touch lifecycle automation for bare metal. Add servers and watch NICo discover, image and provision them through its lifecycle state machine; assign tenants on a shared fabric and see DPU-enforced isolation deny cross-tenant traffic. Includes an explainer film.

modules.title: VXLAN — MAC-in-UDP overlays
modules.description: How a virtual Layer-2 network rides across a routed fabric. Build VNI segments and hosts on a spine-leaf topology, then send a frame and watch the leaf VTEP encapsulate it, route it over the underlay and decapsulate it — with VNI isolation, BGP-EVPN vs flood-and-learn, ARP suppression and a live header-stack inspector. Includes a diagrammatic explainer film.

modules.title: MetalLB — bare-metal load balancer
modules.description: How Kubernetes gets a LoadBalancer Service on bare metal. Define an IPAddressPool, create Services and watch the controller allocate external IPs, then switch the speaker between Layer-2 mode (one elected leader answers ARP/NDP) and BGP mode (every node advertises a /32 and the router load-balances with ECMP). Send traffic and kill nodes to compare failover. Includes a diagrammatic explainer film.

modules.title: VLANs — from the wire up
modules.description: How one switch becomes many isolated networks. Build access ports and a trunk across two switches, watch the 802.1Q tag get pushed on the trunk and stripped at the access port, keep broadcasts inside their VLAN, handle the native VLAN (and its mismatch), and route between VLANs with router-on-a-stick or an L3 switch. Trace any frame hop by hop with its tagged/untagged state, and inject the classic misconfigurations. Includes a diagrammatic explainer film.
